name: The White House
subject: President brings ‘America First’ to Davos
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d98cab711dc3b42af8360f47ff9dd8dfbda1afd0c87583c6b9bf78037b5fc98bc560c88feeb5cebedc1ac846be328c213642c8923b18e084

President brings ‘America First’ to Davos

President Donald J. Trump addressed the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting—more commonly known as “Davos”—this morning to tell global leaders why “America First” won’t mean the rest of the world falls behind.

"Only by insisting on fair and reciprocal trade can we create a system that works not just for the United States but for all nations," the President said. He also made his pitch for investing in the U.S. economy. "America is open for business and we are competitive once again."

Yesterday, President Trump spent his first full day in Switzerland bolstering relationships with key allies. He met with British Prime Minister Theresa May before holding a bilateral meeting with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu later in the afternoon.

While both meetings covered a range of issues, trade was an important common thread.

“We continue to have that really special relationship between the UK and the United States, standing shoulder-to -shoulder because we're facing the same challenges across the world,” Prime Minister May told reporters after meeting with President Trump.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d98cab711dc3b42a2c566610ded865977e2912811831e654c6625010b66afe2ce42b914052359721a92200d8b5ef92f75acc2f2abfcb756e
Watch President Trump’s speech to Davos attendees here.

‘We remember’

Tomorrow will mark 73 years to the day when the Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp was liberated from Nazi control. In recognition of tomorrow’s International Holocaust Remembrance Day, First Lady Melania Trump visited the United States Holocaust Memorial Museum yesterday.

“My thoughts and prayers are with the people whose lives and families were broken by the horrors of the Holocaust,” the First Lady said. “Yet it is also through our shared humanity that we come together now in commemoration, strength, and love.”

The First Lady concluded her tour at the Hall of Remembrance, participating in a moment of silence by the Eternal Flame Memorial followed by a lighting of a candle at the Prayer Wall.

“My heart is with you, and we remember.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d98cab711dc3b42ae3f1d7d0eed737e4aeacb5c788546a52001e26fbddf52d2e5782cf32db06914837dd2874dda97d4b7d94040294f27098
See photos from the First Lady’s visit here.

White House releases framework on immigration

The White House released a framework on immigration reform and border security yesterday to support Congress’ effort in the coming weeks to reach a bipartisan compromise.

The framework lays out in clear detail what President Trump expects for any reform bill to earn his signature:

- Border security: The Department of Homeland Security must have the tools to deter illegal immigration, including physical infrastructure, technology, resources, and personnel.
- DACA legalization: The President hopes for legal status to be extended to DACA recipients and other DACA-eligible illegal immigrants, with a 10-12 year path to citizenship based on requirements for work, education, and character.
- Protect the nuclear family: Congress should promote nuclear family migration by limiting family sponsorships to spouses and minor children.
- Eliminate the lottery and repurpose Visas: The current Visa Lottery system selects individuals at random to come to the United States without consideration of skills, merit, or public safety. Our intake system must better serve the national interest.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d98cab711dc3b42afcd29efee8f968c57cf21943c8ed31c4bba5aa4ea2ca0cf3b5e6650c18b4f1b42320647f7ba694995d1123b891df47bb
See an overview of the framework here.

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump visits the United States Holocaust Memorial Museum | January 25, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will conclude his trip abroad, departing Zurich, Switzerland, en route to Washington, D.C.

This afternoon, Vice President Mike Pence will participate in a National Republican Senatorial Committee Lunch.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d98cab711dc3b42a6f755be39705e0caa5323d50170486b4b2500746d126d4e208f8185fa731cd9d452412ef0b016c9b84a91844d66adef2
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: Keren Charles Dongo
subject: Hey - forwarding you this email from Tim Kaine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek --

I wanted to make sure you saw the email Tim sent earlier this week sharing his petition to take back a Democratic majority in the Senate. The fact is, outnumbering the GOP is the *only* guaranteed way for us to get the real results that American families need.

**And I believe we can do it, but if there's one thing I learned in 2016, it's that we absolutely cannot take anything for granted.** From Corey "MAGA" Stewart up in NoVa to notoriously anti-choice and anti-LGBTQ Virginia delegate Nick Freitas, Republicans are chomping at the bit to replace Tim. Even Congressman Scott Taylor and former Governor Jim Gilmore are reportedly considering jumping into the race.

It's a circus out there -- but whichever one of these guys ends up as our opponent is going to have the backing of national Republicans and special interests like the NRA, so we'll need to be ready to step up and do the work to hold onto Tim's seat.

**Sign his petition today to say you're ready to see a Democratic majority in the Senate:**

[**http://timkaine.com/democratic-majority-petition**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=T%2FaZ%2BR0LshYOmRYo0sGePjI3%2F1emxuSK)

-Keren

Keren Charles Dongo
Campaign Manager
Kaine for Virginia 2018

_\---------- Forwarded message ----------_
From: **Tim Kaine**
Subject: Add your name for a Democratic majority: Over the past few months, it has become increasingly clear that much of our current government just isn't working for the people of this country. It's gridlocked, it's dysfunctional, and President Trump and Republican leaders spent last year focused on taking away health care and giving tax cuts to those at the very top, instead of solving the challenges facing our country. So many Virginians and Americans are feeling dismayed by it -- I know I sure am some days.

**But we can move forward. How?** [**By taking back a Democratic majority this November.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=eCY6VsJDsiFxElfofxksazI3%2F1emxuSK) On November 6th, 34 of our total 100 Senate seats will be up. Right now, Republicans hold a slim 51-seat majority. That means if we hold our seats and pick up just two seats, we can get the results our country needs. We can expand, not repeal, health care. We can permanently protect Dreamers. We can fix the disastrous tax bill that benefits those at the top and leaves the middle class holding the bag.

[**Add your name if you're ready for a Democratic majority in the Senate.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=%2BiOkt0dtDji6AX%2FsgvRxUzI3%2F1emxuSK)

**This is the reality we're trying to change: We have a president sitting in the Oval Office pushing an extreme agenda and far too many Republicans in Congress, including the Senate and House leaders, who are unwilling to stand up to him.** We need a real emergency brake on this runaway train -- and by taking back a Democratic majority, Congress has the capacity to be that check on Donald Trump and his most outrageous, dangerous ideas.

2018 is our chance. I know we can do it: Look at Democrats' sweep in Virginia this past fall (and the fifteen House of Delegates seats we flipped!). Look at my friend Doug Jones, the first Democrat to be elected to the Senate from Alabama in 25 years. Look at the incredible anniversary of the Women's March just this past weekend. Look at the activism and organizing we're seeing across the country every single day.

[**Sign my petition right now to say you're going to fight for a Democratic majority in the Senate on November 6th, 2018.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=0oif7aJjdc%2BnsAKItT4%2BhDI3%2F1emxuSK)

Thanks for all you do. I'll be right beside you in this fight.

Tim Kaine

| | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=MEbtJATeA3KKPrccN3Et5DI3%2F1emxuSK)

---

name: Jason Yates
subject: Huge pro-life victory on the horizon
body:
| | If we act...
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2F5LK9Cr0&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180126p2a01&n=1)
| Peter, Your voice is needed. The U.S. House passed the "Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act” on October 3rd of last year. And now the United States Senate is expected to vote on this important bill this Monday, January 29th! The bill is based on the premise that unborn babies can feel pain at 20 weeks and as Senator Lindsey Graham says because of medical advancements “viability has definitely changed since 1973.” If the bill passes in the United States Senate all abortions after 20 weeks gestation will be banned. Exceptions are made for cases of maternal mortality risk or cases of rape or incest against a minor. This bill wouldn’t protect all unborn life but it would be a huge step in the right direction that would no doubt save hundreds of thousands of lives every year. We need 60 votes to get this bill past a filibuster in the Senate and sent to the President’s desk. Unlike the last pro-abortion Presidential administration, President Trump has already said he would sign this bill into law. The only obstacle that remains is getting enough Senators to vote for life. **Now is the time for Senators from both parties to hear from _you_ and millions of other Christians across the nation. ** The right to life is the most basic and fundamental to all human rights and Christians must stand boldly on this issue. We need to contact our Senators and ask them to vote in support of the “Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act” to protect the most basic of all human rights - the right to live. **[We’ve made it super easy - you just need to use our quick tool to send a message to your Senators now!](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2F5LK9Cr0&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180126p2a01&n=2)** Take action and then spread the word to your friends and family encouraging them to take action. Now is our chance. We only have until Monday to contact our Senators before the vote! [](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2F5LK9Cr0&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180126p2a01&n=3) Together, 90-million Christians strong, our voice is loud and powerful. **United. We Stand.**

Jason Yates
CEO
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180126p2a01&n=4)

Your input will help my campaign build a Hoosier-based policy platform that will help President Trump advance his agenda and fix Washington.

subject: Sen. Kamala Harris is sick and tired of Republicans playing politics withwomen’s health
body:
Dear Activist,

Republican leaders in the Senate have scheduled a vote Monday on legislation that would enact an unconstitutional 20-week abortion ban, just one week after the Trump Administration introduced new rules granting health care workers the license to discriminate against women seeking an abortion.

Here’s the truth: I am sick and tired of Republican politicians playing politics with our health care and going out of their way to target women’s constitutional right to choose in the process. We deserve better, which is why I’m asking you to take action:

Tell Republican leaders and my colleagues in Congress to stop attacking women’s health and reproductive rights, and vote down this ban >> (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/ads-180125-abortion-ban?source=ads_dems_180126_all_ad1)

SIGN THE PETITION (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/ads-180125-abortion-ban?source=ads_dems_180126_all_ad1) I’ve spent my career in office fighting those who want to roll back decades’ worth of progress on women’s health and reproductive freedom..

Together we fought to require employers to provide employees with no-cost coverage for contraception and 55 million American women benefited.

Together we advanced legislation in my state of California to ensure that so-called crisis pregnancy centers provide women with accurate information about the full range of reproductive services available to them.

Together we defeated every attempt by Republicans to defund Planned Parenthood since Trump’s election, and we’re never going to stop fighting to make sure those doors stay open and women get the care they need.

And next week, we’re going to defeat this 20-week abortion ban if enough of us make our voices heard right now:

Sign my petition today if you agree that the Republicans 20-week abortion ban is immoral and must be defeated in the Senate next week. (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/ads-180125-abortion-ban?source=ads_dems_180126_all_ad1)

Now is the time we must keep fighting to defend and expand on the progress made for women’s rights and reproductive health, not fall backward.

Thanks for all you do.

Kamala Harris
U.S. Senator, California

SIGN THE PETITION (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/ads-180125-abortion-ban?source=ads_dems_180126_all_ad1)

subject: Sorry this is long, but it's important:
body:
Things aren't going to get better in this country until we take back power from the Republicans.
---
Derek --

I don't think there's such a thing as an easy week in Donald Trump's America, but this sure has seemed like one of the harder ones.

In tough times, it can be exhausting to stay focused -- it seems easier to give up and check out. But that's the last thing we can afford to do right now. In fact, it's exactly what they want us to do.

When it comes down to it, this is all pretty simple:
1. Republicans have unified control of the federal government.
2. As a result of that, some terrible things have been happening in this country.
3. The best way to do something about all of this is to take Republicans' power from them by electing more Democrats.

**That's why I'm asking for your help today, Derek. Can you make a donation of $3 right now and help Democrats fight back against Republicans?**

[DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e50601c/2215090977/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e50601d/2215090977/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e50601a/2215090977/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e50601b/2215090977/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506018/2215090977/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506019/2215090977/VEsHBw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")
There has been some good news this week that makes me even more optimistic about our chances to deliver a devastating blow to Republicans this year:
* Last weekend, millions of people across the country came out to Women's March events -- an incredible show of grassroots energy and enthusiasm.
* The Pennsylvania Supreme Court struck down the state's outrageously gerrymandered congressional map, putting more House seats firmly in play in that state.
* An _ABC News_ poll shows Democrats holding a **14-point lead** over Republicans among likely voters in the generic congressional ballot -- an indicator of a potential wave election this November.

The next few months are going to be hard, Derek. But if we stick together and fight back, we'll be in a position to take back power from Republicans in Congress, in state houses, and in city halls all across this country.

**That's how we'll get our country back on track -- and it starts with people like you showing that you're in to win right now. If you're with us, donate $3 right now to show it:**

**[https://my.democrats.org/Stay-Focused](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506026/2215090977/VEsHBg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=13+-+httpsmydemocratsorgStayFocused&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")**

Let's go,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

P.S. -- Things aren't going to get better in this country until we take back power from the Republicans. **[Help Democrats get our country back on track by chipping in $3 today.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506027/2215090977/VEsHAQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tp-stay-focused?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=14+-+Help+Democrats+get+our+country+back+on+t&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd")**

Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506025/2215090977/VEsHAw/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=16+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506022/2215090977/VEsHAg/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=17+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522201/4067b0a/4be0ad107/7e506023/2215090977/VEsHDQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=18+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180126_tp_nd&source=em_20180126_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180126_tp_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

subject: Running out of time

body:
Hi there,

I just received another update from my team. We're within striking distance to flipping another Legislative seat in a local Special Election, but I need your help!

The Florida Democratic Party has momentum, but before we head into November, we need to elect Democrat Margaret Good for State House in a critical Special Election.

[**Help us take this Blue Wave to Sarasota by supporting Margaret Good today and donate $5 before midnight tonight >>>**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4bf49/1a5213b3/7219a003/718b0be4/1321252733/VEsH/ "https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-7119830304984003840?utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+Help+us+take+this+Blue+Wave+to+Sarasota+&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180126_1_2&source=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180126_1_2&ms=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180125_1_1&amtOpts=3%2C5%2C10%2C25%2C50%2C100&am=5")

Margaret knows it's time to shake things up in Tallahassee and fight for the health care and jobs we need, the education our children deserve, and the environment we love.

[**Help us flip this seat in a Special Election. Click here and rush a donation before our midnight deadline >>>**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4bf49/1a5213b3/7219a003/718b0be7/1321252733/VEsE/ "https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-7119830304984003840?utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+Help+us+flip+this+seat+in+a+Special+Elec&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180126_1_2&source=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180126_1_2&ms=FDP_EM_FR_180DO_180125_1_1&amtOpts=3%2C5%2C10%2C25%2C50%2C100&am=5")

Let's continue the Blue Wave,

Terrie Rizzo

body:
Governor Eric Greitens announced the principles for his plan for state tax reform. He will cut taxes for families, lower taxes for businesses, end loopholes, and do it all in a way that is fiscally sound.

Team,

Governor Greitens has announced his plan to tackle tax reform for all Missourians. He will cut taxes for working families, lower taxes for businesses, and end tax loopholes -- all in a way that is fiscally sound.

This revenue-neutral plan is responsible and bold. It cuts taxes for more than 97% of Missourians.

Next Monday and Tuesday, the Governor will travel the state to talk about his plans in further detail. We hope to see you at one of our stops on the Tax Tour.

body:

---

| | **_The Washington Post_ : Ryan Says Republicans to Target Medicare in 2018
[Pitch in to stop Republicans from gutting Medicare >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c220f/7377f838/33211502/51fa0113/1717024262/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-medicaregop?refcode=20180126.2nd&amount=10")** Peter,

When Paul Ryan laid out the Republicans' agenda for 2018, gutting Medicare was at the top of his list. This isn't anything new -- he's been trying to slash funding for seniors' health care for decades to finance more tax cuts for the wealthy. We've always been able to put a stop to it, but _now that Republicans have total control of the federal government, Medicare is on the chopping block once again -- and two of Joe 's likely opponents are already fighting for Ryan's cuts._

**Congressman Luke Messer and Congressman Todd Rokita have _already shown they want_ to cut Medicare by voting for Mitch McConnell's tax plan without trying to stop billions of dollars worth of cuts in Medicare in the bill.** If they can't stand up to Ryan in the House, there's no way they'll be able to fight back in the Senate when the stakes are higher for Hoosiers. **And that's what Mitch McConnell is counting on.** | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c220f/7377f838/33211502/51fa0113/1717024262/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-medicaregop?refcode=20180126.2nd&amount=10")

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, JANUARY 26th**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**In case you haven’t heard, it’s National School Choice Week. All across the country folks are celebrating and promoting the concept that a young person’s access to high quality education shouldn’t be constrained by their zip code.**
****
**National School Choice Week was founded in 2011 with the idea that all forms of school choice should be available, whether it's district schools, charter schools, home schooling, or private schools. One form of educational choice is not promoted over another, rather school choice proponents believe parents know what educational situation is best for their child and they should be able to choose.**
****
**Here in New Hampshire, we have much to celebrate! With a Governor who makes education a number one priority and a Republican-led legislature that believes the same. You don’t have to look far to see the successes we’ve achieved in the last year:**
****
* **Governor appoints Frank Edelblut as Commissioner of Education**
* **Governor establishes a Governor's scholarship fund to increase access to higher education for New Hampshire students**
* **Governor advocates for innovative reforms that local school boards can implement**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, JANUARY 26th | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, In case you haven’t heard, it’s National School Choice Week. All across the country folks are celebrating and promoting the concept that a young person’s access to high quality education shouldn’t be constrained by their zip code. ** National School Choice Week was founded in 2011 with the idea that all forms of school choice should be available, whether it's district schools, charter schools, home schooling, or private schools. One form of educational choice is not promoted over another, rather school choice proponents believe parents know what educational situation is best for their child and they should be able to choose. Here in New Hampshire, we have much to celebrate! With a Governor who makes education a number one priority and a Republican-led legislature that believes the same. You don’t have to look far to see the successes we’ve achieved in the last year: Governor appoints Frank Edelblut as Commissioner of Education Governor establishes a Governor's scholarship fund to increase access to higher education for New Hampshire students Governor advocates for innovative reforms that local school boards can implement Governor backs Educational Savings Accounts in order to increase choice options for New Hampshire families So today, Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation.
